dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won the day on repeated developer-use praise: one major LLM thread described Claude Code as effectively acting like a helpful locked-down "sysadmin," and another front-page discussion had a solo developer describing Claude subagents as useful for parallel product/design perspectives [E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic led because Hacker News discussion contained the most direct and repeated product-level approval for a tracked provider. The positive signals were not just generic AI excitement; they were concrete descriptions of Claude-based workflows being useful in day-to-day development and product thinking [E2][E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: ChatGPT was used as the reference point for how fast the field has moved, and commenters emphasized current practical value plus willingness to pay for premium access rather than dismissing it as hype [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude-related comments focused on concrete developer utility: Claude Code was described as a constrained but very helpful coding/sysadmin assistant, and another thread described Claude subagents as useful for getting multiple product perspectives in parallel [E2][E3].

evidence.summary: In the front-page 'Year in LLMs' discussion, ChatGPT was cited as the marker of recent acceleration and commenters argued current AI already has clear value, pointing to paid demand for premium access.
A top-level comment in the 'Year in LLMs' thread described running Claude Code under a restricted agent user and said giving it a VPS felt like having a useful sysadmin.

In the 'Build Software. Build Users' thread, a commenter said they created Claude subagents for different perspectives such as customer, design, growth, and technical review, using them in parallel as a practical solo-dev workflow.
